rationale.R1.3: This is the literal load-bearing task: the delegation instructs verification that each claim is supported by the reported numbers. The artifact makes at least one critical evidence-claim inversion — FlowPhot's Cramér–von Mises statistic W²=0.084 is larger (worse) than the baseline's W²=0.031, yet the text asserts this as a 'favorable' result confirming superior calibration. The η_0.15 and σ_NMAD comparisons do directionally support the point estimates, but the ~30% improvement figure is an unsourced arithmetic conflation of two different metrics. R1.3 is the axis on which the delegation rises or falls.
rationale.R1.2: Checking logical consistency is necessary to detect the PIT reasoning failure: the inferential step from 'W²=0.084 vs 0.031' to 'FlowPhot posteriors are closer to ideal uniform' contradicts the definition of the Cramér–von Mises statistic (lower = better). Identifying this requires evaluating whether the intermediate reasoning from evidence to conclusion is valid, not just whether numbers are present.
